title: Reverse Linked List
problem_statement: Reverse a singly linked list and return the reversed list.

examples.explanation: The linked list is reversed in-place.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 5000]
intuition: Iteratively reverse pointers by walking through the list while tracking the previous node.

approaches.name: Iterative pointer reversal
approaches.description: Use three pointers to reverse the list in a single pass with O(1) extra space.
approaches.why_not: A naive recursive approach uses extra stack space and is unnecessary here.

interview_qa.a: Previous, current, and next pointers are enough to reverse the list.
Yes, by changing next pointers without creating new nodes.
